heading: Representative payees
label: 1007 Representative payees
notes: References in TextThe Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in subsec. (b)(2)(B), (3), is classified generally to Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.
Prior ProvisionsFor prior provisions, see note set out under .section 1001 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: If the Commissioner of Social Security determines that the interest of any qualified individual under this subchapter would be served thereby, payment of the qualified individual’s benefit under this subchapter may be made, regardless of the legal competency or incompetency of the qualified individual, either directly to the qualified individual, or for his or her use and benefit, to another person (the meaning of which term, for purposes of this section, includes an organization) with respect to whom the requirements of subsection (b) have been met (in this section referred to as the qualified individual’s “representative payee”). If the Commissioner of Social Security determines that a representative payee has misused any benefit paid to the representative payee pursuant to this section, 405(j) of this title, or , the Commissioner of Social Security shall promptly revoke the person’s designation as the qualified individual’s representative payee under this subsection, and shall make payment to an alternative representative payee or, if the interest of the qualified individual under this subchapter would be served thereby, to the qualified individual.section 1383(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Examination of fitness of prospective representative payee
children.label: b Examination of fitness of prospective representative payee
A
children.text: an investigation by the Commissioner of Social Security of the person to serve as representative payee, which shall be conducted in advance of the determination and shall, to the extent practicable, include a face-to-face interview with the person (or, in the case of an organization, a representative of the organization); and

children.label: B
children.text: adequate evidence that the arrangement is in the interest of the qualified individual.

children.label: A
children.text: require the person being investigated to submit documented proof of the identity of the person;

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a person who has a social security account number issued for purposes of the program under subchapter II or an employer identification number issued for purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, verify the number;

children.label: C
children.text: determine whether the person has been convicted of a violation of section 408, 1011, or 1383a of this title;

children.label: D
children.text: obtain information concerning whether such person has been convicted of any other offense under Federal or State law which resulted in imprisonment for more than 1 year;

children.label: E
children.text: obtain information concerning whether such person is a person described in ;section 1004(a)(2) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: determine whether payment of benefits to the person in the capacity as representative payee has been revoked or terminated pursuant to this section, , or by reason of misuse of funds paid as benefits under this subchapter, subchapter II, or XVI, respectively, andsection 405(j) of this titlesection 1383(a)(2)(A)(iii) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: determine whether such person has been convicted (and not subsequently exonerated), under Federal or State law, of a felony provided under paragraph (4), or of an attempt or a conspiracy to commit such a felony.

children.label: A
children.text: such person is described in ,section 1004(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: such person has information that is necessary for the officer to conduct the officer’s official duties, and

children.label: C
children.text: the location or apprehension of such person is within the officer’s official duties.

children.label: A
children.text: Human trafficking, including as prohibited under sections 1590 and 1591 of title 18.

children.label: B
children.text: False imprisonment, including as prohibited under .section 1201 of title 18

children.label: C
children.text: Kidnapping, including as prohibited under .section 1201 of title 18

children.label: D
children.text: Rape and sexual assault, including as prohibited under sections 2241, 2242, 2243, and 2244 of title 18.

children.label: E
children.text: First-degree homicide, including as prohibited under .section 1111 of title 18

children.label: F
children.text: Robbery, including as prohibited under .section 2111 of title 18

children.label: G
children.text: Fraud to obtain access to government assistance, including as prohibited under sections 287, 1001, and 1343 of title 18.

children.label: H
children.text: Fraud by scheme, including as prohibited under .section 1343 of title 18

children.label: I
children.text: Theft of government funds or property, including as prohibited under .section 641 of title 18

children.label: J
children.text: Abuse or neglect, including as prohibited under sections 111, 113, 114, 115, 116, or 117 of title 18.

children.label: K
children.text: Forgery, including as prohibited under section 642 and chapter 25 (except section 512) of title 18.

children.label: L
children.text: Identity theft or identity fraud, including as prohibited under sections 1028 and 1028A of title 18.

children.label: A
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out the activities required under paragraph (2) as part of the investigation under paragraph (1)(A), the Commissioner may conduct a background check of any individual seeking to serve as a representative payee under this subsection and may disqualify from service as a representative payee any such individual who fails to grant permission for the Commissioner to conduct such a background check.

children.label: B
children.text: The Commissioner may revoke certification of payment of benefits under this subsection to any individual serving as a representative payee on or after who fails to grant permission for the Commissioner to conduct such a background check.January 1, 2019

children.heading: Requirement for maintaining lists of undesirable payees
children.label: c Requirement for maintaining lists of undesirable payees

children.text: the names and (if issued) social security account numbers or employer identification numbers of all persons with respect to whom, in the capacity of representative payee, the payment of benefits has been revoked or terminated under this section, , or by reason of misuse of funds paid as benefits under this subchapter, subchapter II, or XVI, respectively; andsection 405(j) of this titlesection 1383(a)(2)(A)(iii) of this title
the names and (if issued) social security account numbers or employer identification numbers of all persons who have been convicted of a violation of section 408, 1011, or 1383a of this title.

children.heading: Persons ineligible to serve as representative payees
children.label: d Persons ineligible to serve as representative payees
A
children.text: the person has been convicted of a violation of section 408, 1011, or 1383a of this title;

children.label: B
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (2), payment of benefits to the person in the capacity of representative payee has been revoked or terminated under this section, , or section 1383(a)(2)(A)(ii) of this title by reason of misuse of funds paid as benefits under this subchapter, subchapter II, or subchapter XVI, respectively;section 405(j) of this title1So in original. Probably should be “1383(a)(2)(A)(iii)”.1

children.label: C
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (2)(B), the person is a creditor of the qualified individual and provides the qualified individual with goods or services for consideration;

children.label: D
children.text: such person has previously been convicted as described in subsection (b)(2)(D), unless the Commissioner determines that such payment would be appropriate notwithstanding such conviction;

children.label: E
children.text: such person is a person described in ,section 1004(a)(2) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: except as provided in paragraph (2)(D), such person has previously been convicted (and not subsequently exonerated) as described in subsection (b)(2)(G), or

children.label: G
children.text: such person’s benefits under this subchapter, subchapter II, or subchapter XVI are certified for payment to a representative payee during the period for which the individual’s benefits would be certified for payment to another person.

children.label: A
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security may prescribe circumstances under which the Commissioner of Social Security may grant an exemption from paragraph (1) to any person on a case-by-case basis if the exemption is in the best interest of the qualified individual whose benefits would be paid to the person pursuant to this section.

children.label: i
children.text: a relative of the qualified individual and the relative resides in the same household as the qualified individual;

children.label: ii
children.text: a legal guardian or legal representative of the individual;

children.label: iii
children.text: a facility that is licensed or certified as a care facility under the law of the political jurisdiction in which the qualified individual resides;

children.label: iv
children.text: a person who is an administrator, owner, or employee of a facility referred to in clause (iii), if the qualified individual resides in the facility, and the payment to the facility or the person is made only after the Commissioner of Social Security has made a good faith effort to locate an alternative representative payee to whom payment would serve the best interests of the qualified individual; or

children.label: v
children.text: a person who is determined by the Commissioner of Social Security, on the basis of written findings and pursuant to procedures prescribed by the Commissioner of Social Security, to be acceptable to serve as a representative payee.

children.label: i
children.text: the person poses no risk to the qualified individual;

children.label: ii
children.text: the financial relationship of the person to the qualified individual poses no substantial conflict of interest; and

children.label: iii
children.text: no other more suitable representative payee can be found.

children.label: I
children.text: subsection (b)(2)(G) shall not apply; and

children.label: II
children.text: the Commissioner may grant an exemption from the provisions of paragraph (1)(F) if the Commissioner determines that such exemption is in the best interest of the individual entitled to benefits.

children.label: I
children.text: is the custodial spouse of the beneficiary for whom the person applies to serve;

children.label: II
children.text: is the custodial court appointed guardian of the beneficiary for whom the person applies to serve; or

children.label: III
children.text: received a presidential or gubernatorial pardon for the relevant conviction.

children.heading: Deferral of payment pending appointment of representative payee
children.label: e Deferral of payment pending appointment of representative payee

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), if the Commissioner of Social Security makes a determination described in the first sentence of subsection (a) with respect to any qualified individual’s benefit and determines that direct payment of the benefit to the qualified individual would cause substantial harm to the qualified individual, the Commissioner of Social Security may defer (in the case of initial entitlement) or suspend (in the case of existing entitlement) direct payment of the benefit to the qualified individual, until such time as the selection of a representative payee is made pursuant to this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), any deferral or suspension of direct payment of a benefit pursuant to paragraph (1) shall be for a period of not more than 1 month.

children.heading: Exception in the case of incompetency
children.label: B Exception in the case of incompetency
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply in any case in which the qualified individual is, as of the date of the Commissioner of Social Security’s determination, legally incompetent under the laws of the jurisdiction in which the individual resides.

children.heading: Payment of retroactive benefits
children.label: 3 Payment of retroactive benefits
children.text: Payment of any benefits which are deferred or suspended pending the selection of a representative payee shall be made to the qualified individual or the representative payee as a single sum or over such period of time as the Commissioner of Social Security determines is in the best interest of the qualified individual.

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: f Hearing
children.text: Any qualified individual who is dissatisfied with a determination by the Commissioner of Social Security to make payment of the qualified individual’s benefit to a representative payee under subsection (a) of this section or with the designation of a particular person to serve as representative payee shall be entitled to a hearing by the Commissioner of Social Security to the same extent as is provided in , and to judicial review of the Commissioner of Social Security’s final decision as is provided in .section 1009(a) of this titlesection 1009(b) of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In advance, to the extent practicable, of the payment of a qualified individual’s benefit to a representative payee under subsection (a), the Commissioner of Social Security shall provide written notice of the Commissioner’s initial determination to so make the payment. The notice shall be provided to the qualified individual, except that, if the qualified individual is legally incompetent, then the notice shall be provided solely to the legal guardian or legal representative of the qualified individual.

children.label: A
children.text: to appeal a determination that a representative payee is necessary for the qualified individual;

children.label: B
children.text: to appeal the designation of a particular person to serve as the representative payee of the qualified individual; and

children.label: C
children.text: to review the evidence upon which the designation is based and to submit additional evidence.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In any case where payment under this subchapter is made to a person other than the qualified individual entitled to the payment, the Commissioner of Social Security shall establish a system of accountability monitoring under which the person shall report not less often than annually with respect to the use of the payments. The Commissioner of Social Security shall establish and implement statistically valid procedures for reviewing the reports in order to identify instances in which persons are not properly using the payments.

children.heading: Special reports
children.label: 2 Special reports
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), the Commissioner of Social Security may require a report at any time from any person receiving payments on behalf of a qualified individual, if the Commissioner of Social Security has reason to believe that the person receiving the payments is misusing the payments.

children.label: A
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply in any case where the other person to whom such payment is made is the spouse of the individual entitled to such payment.

children.label: B
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall establish and implement procedures as necessary for the Commissioner to determine the eligibility of such parties for the exemption provided in subparagraph (A). The Commissioner shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to determine eligibility for such exemption.

children.heading: Authority to redirect delivery of benefit payments when a representative payee fails to provide required accounting
children.label: 4 Authority to redirect delivery of benefit payments when a representative payee fails to provide required accounting
children.text: In any case in which the person described in paragraph (1) or (2) receiving benefit payments on behalf of a qualified individual fails to submit a report required by the Commissioner of Social Security under paragraph (1) or (2), the Commissioner may, after furnishing notice to such person and the qualified individual, require that such person appear in person at a United States Government facility designated by the Social Security Administration as serving the area in which the qualified individual resides in order to receive such benefit payments.

children.label: A
children.text: the name, address, and (if issued) the social security account number or employer identification number of each representative payee who is receiving benefit payments pursuant to this section, , or ; andsection 405(j) of this titlesection 1383(a)(2) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the name, address, and social security account number of each individual for whom each representative payee is reported to be providing services as representative payee pursuant to this section, , or .section 405(j) of this titlesection 1383(a)(2) of this title

children.heading: Maintaining lists of agencies
children.label: 6 Maintaining lists of agencies
children.text: The Commissioner of Social Security shall maintain lists, which shall be updated periodically, of public agencies and community-based nonprofit social service agencies which are qualified to serve as representative payees pursuant to this section and which are located in the jurisdiction in which any qualified individual resides.

children.label: A
children.text: is not an individual; or

children.label: B
children.text: is an individual who, for any month during a period when misuse occurs, serves 15 or more individuals who are beneficiaries under this subchapter, subchapter II, subchapter XVI, or any combination of such subchapters;

children.heading: Misuse of benefits
children.label: j Misuse of benefits
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, misuse of benefits by a representative payee occurs in any case in which the representative payee receives payment under this subchapter for the use and benefit of another person under this subchapter and converts such payment, or any part thereof, to a use other than for the use and benefit of such person. The Commissioner of Social Security may prescribe by regulation the meaning of the term “use and benefit” for purposes of this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: the representative payee is a person who serves in that capacity with respect to 15 or more such individuals; or

children.label: B
children.text: the representative payee is an agency that serves in that capacity with respect to 50 or more such individuals.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of such reviews;

children.label: B
children.text: the results of such reviews;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of cases in which the representative payee was changed and why;

children.label: D
children.text: the number of cases involving the exercise of expedited, targeted oversight of the representative payee by the Commissioner conducted upon receipt of an allegation of misuse of funds, failure to pay a vendor, or a similar irregularity;

children.label: E
children.text: the number of cases discovered in which there was a misuse of funds;

children.label: F
children.text: how any such cases of misuse of funds were dealt with by the Commissioner;

children.label: G
children.text: the final disposition of such cases of misuse of funds, including any criminal penalties imposed; and

children.label: H
children.text: such other information as the Commissioner deems appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If the Commissioner of Social Security or a court of competent jurisdiction determines that a representative payee that is not a Federal, State, or local government agency has misused all or part of a qualified individual’s benefit that was paid to such representative payee under this section, the representative payee shall be liable for the amount misused, and such amount (to the extent not repaid by the representative payee) shall be treated as an overpayment of benefits under this subchapter to the representative payee for all purposes of this chapter and related laws pertaining to the recovery of such overpayments. Subject to paragraph (2), upon recovering all or any part of such amount, the Commissioner shall make payment of an amount equal to the recovered amount to such qualified individual or such qualified individual’s alternative representative payee.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The total of the amount paid to such individual or such individual’s alternative representative payee under paragraph (1) and the amount paid under subsection (i) may not exceed the total benefit amount misused by the representative payee with respect to such individual.
